# Call Prompter — Context for LLMs and AI Agents

## What this project does

Call Prompter is a real-time AI sales co-pilot for Google Meet calls. It captures live captions via Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP), analyzes conversation chunks every 15 seconds using an LLM, and streams actionable sales insights to a terminal-style WebSocket UI.

## Architecture

```
Chrome (Google Meet with captions enabled)
  → Chrome CDP (port 9222) reads caption DOM elements every 15s
  → server.mjs deduplicates chunks, maintains conversation history
  → Claude CLI analyzes each chunk with full context
  → WebSocket (port 4242) broadcasts structured JSON messages
  → ui.html renders insights in real-time (color-coded by type)
```

## File structure

| File | Purpose | Language |
|------|---------|----------|
| server.mjs | WebSocket server, CDP caption capture, LLM analysis loop, demo mode | JavaScript (ESM) |
| ui.html | Terminal-style prompter UI, sidebar, controls, responsive | HTML/CSS/JS (single file) |
| watch.mjs | Auto-detect Google Meet tabs via CDP, auto-start/stop prompter | JavaScript (ESM) |
| setup.mjs | Configure Chrome to launch with CDP flag (Win/Mac/Linux) | JavaScript (ESM) |
| cli.mjs | CLI entry point, routes to server or watch mode | JavaScript (ESM) |

## Key concepts

1. **CDP Caption Capture**: Connects to Chrome's remote debugging protocol, finds Meet tabs, evaluates JS to extract caption DOM elements (selectors: `[jsname="tgaKEf"]`, `[data-message-text]`, `.iOzk7`, `.TBMuR span`).

2. **Chunk Analysis**: Each 15s chunk is sent to Claude with conversation history (last 10 chunks) and prospect context. Claude returns structured JSON with: keywords, sentiment, suggestion, objection/response, budget signals, closing opportunity, danger alerts.

3. **WebSocket Message Types**: `transcript`, `insight`, `suggestion`, `closing`, `danger`, `objection`, `sentiment`, `keyword`, `budget`, `system`.

4. **Demo Mode**: `--demo` flag runs without Chrome/CDP, cycles through realistic sales scenarios for testing the UI.

5. **Watch Mode**: Polls `http://127.0.0.1:9222/json` every 5s for `meet.google.com` tabs. Auto-starts prompter when detected, stops when tab closes.

## How to modify

- **Change LLM**: Replace the `claude -p` call in `analyzeChunk()` (server.mjs) with any LLM CLI or API call. The prompt expects JSON output.
- **Change analysis interval**: Modify `CAPTURE_INTERVAL_MS` (default 15000ms).
- **Add new insight types**: Add a case in `handleMessage()` (ui.html) and corresponding CSS class.
- **Change Meet caption selectors**: Update the `expression` string in `captureCaption()` if Google changes their DOM.
- **Add new platforms**: Fork `captureCaption()` to support Zoom, Teams, etc. by targeting their caption DOM elements.

## Dependencies

- Runtime: Bun >= 1.0 or Node.js >= 20
- Chrome with `--remote-debugging-port=9222`
- Claude Code CLI (for LLM analysis in live mode)
- No npm packages required (uses built-in WebSocket from Bun)

## Common agent tasks

- "Add support for Zoom": Create a new capture function targeting Zoom's caption DOM, add platform detection in the capture loop.
- "Use GPT instead of Claude": Replace `execFileSync('claude', ...)` in `analyzeChunk()` with an OpenAI API call. Keep the same JSON output format.
- "Add a new insight type": Add the type to the analysis prompt, add a `case` in `handleMessage()`, add CSS styling.
- "Make it work without Chrome": Implement audio capture via system audio device + Whisper STT instead of CDP caption scraping.
- "Deploy as a web service": Serve ui.html via the WebSocket server (add HTTP handler), containerize with Docker.
